speaker: speaker1
text: did you know that abortion is still illegal in many states in the USA ?

speaker: speaker2
text: why ? I thought Americans were supposed to be more open than that .

speaker: speaker1
text: well , for many religious people , they believe that people who abort their babies are murderers .

speaker: speaker2
text: do you think most people in America believe that abortion is wrong ?

speaker: speaker1
text: no , I think it's mostly the older generation who are against abortions .

speaker: speaker2
text: it's no wonder there are so many teenage pregnancies in America .

speaker: speaker1
text: religious people blame it on pre-marital sex , not on abortion being illegal .

speaker: speaker2
text: what do you think about abortion ?

speaker: speaker1
text: I personally would never want to have one , but I think that since a pregnancy can affect a woman's entire life , she should have the right to decide for herself .

speaker: speaker2
text: do you think the fathers should have some kind of say in the matter ?

speaker: speaker1
text: I know this is going to sound sexist , but I don't think that men should have to give their approval to the mothers of their children .

speaker: speaker2
text: why not ? It's their children , too .

speaker: speaker1
text: that may be true , but when a woman gets pregnant , it changes her life completely . Men can still carry on with their lives after the birth exactly as they did beforehand .

speaker: speaker2
text: that might be true , but I think men should have some kind of say in the matter .
